metadata.title: Bizarre Lineage Stats Guide: Build Logic & Priorities
metadata.description: Learn how to approach Bizarre Lineage stats with better early-game priorities, cleaner stand build logic, and fewer wasted point allocations.

hero.title: Stats should support your route, not trap you in fake precision.

aside.title: How to read this page
aside.description: The guide explains what each stat affects, what early players should prioritize, and where common build mistakes come from.

overview.title: Each stat category should answer a different build question.

priorities.title: Early stat choices should create stable progress first.

mistakes.title: Most weak builds fail because the logic is wrong, not because the math is slightly off.

related.title: Use the stats guide as build context, not as an isolated page.

tier_list.title: Tier List
tier_list.description: Check what role your stand is trying to play before you decide how to support it.

prestige.title: Prestige Guide
prestige.description: Prestige timing becomes cleaner when you understand what parts of your build matter now.

beginner.title: Beginner Guide
beginner.description: Use the new-player route if you still need the higher-level progression sequence.

faq.title: What the stats page should clarify.

content.overview: Stats should explain decision logic, not pretend every player needs a perfect spreadsheet. The goal is to support your stand, route, and mode preference without trapping yourself in a wasteful allocation pattern.

stat_cards.title: Power stats
stat_cards.description: These typically shape your direct damage ceiling and whether your build can capitalize on openings.

stat_cards.title: Durability stats
stat_cards.description: Survivability matters when your route values stability over highlight damage.

stat_cards.title: Mobility and tempo stats
stat_cards.description: These affect how often you can start or escape engagements instead of just how hard you hit.

stat_cards.title: Utility allocation
stat_cards.description: Some points exist to smooth progression rather than maximize a duel scenario.

priorities_list.title: Support the stand you actually use
priorities_list.description: Do not copy a build logic that assumes a different stand, different style, or different goal.

priorities_list.title: Bias toward stable progression early
priorities_list.description: Early-game stat choices should make PvE and general grinding easier before you optimize for niche fights.

priorities_list.title: Separate PvP and PvE thinking
priorities_list.description: The stat mix that wins duels is not always the same mix that keeps progression efficient.

priorities_list.title: Leave room for correction
priorities_list.description: If you are uncertain, avoid locking yourself into an extreme distribution too early.

mistakes_cards.title: Blindly copying endgame builds
mistakes_cards.description: Endgame advice can be actively inefficient on a fresh or mid-game account.

mistakes_cards.title: Overbuilding for one matchup
mistakes_cards.description: A narrow duel-focused setup can make the rest of your route worse.

mistakes_cards.title: Ignoring progression context
mistakes_cards.description: Stats should serve your current grind stage, not just your ideal future state.

faq.question: What should beginners prioritize with stats?
faq.answer: Stable progression, survivability, and a build that supports the stand they can actually use right now.

faq.question: Should PvP and PvE use the same stat logic?
faq.answer: Not always. PvP often rewards sharper specialization, while PvE usually rewards smoother consistency.

faq.question: Why does this guide avoid fake exact numbers?
faq.answer: Because the page is meant to teach allocation thinking that survives patches and different account states.
